In 2022, he made history to become the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress. Maxwell isn't taking any corporate PAC, big sugar, or fossil fuel contributions. He needs your help to protect this seat for poor and working-class people. Will you join our historic movement today? | ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ | PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS Maxwell Frost for Congress PO Box 772671 Orlando, FL 32877-2671 United States | Email is an important way for us to communicate with our supporters. name: Chris Pappas
subject: I'm sorry about Karoline Leavitt
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt ]Chris Pappas for Senate

I’m sorry about Karoline
Leavitt.

Before she was hand-picked as Donald Trump’s White House Press Secretary,
Karoline ran against me for Congress here in New Hampshire. She lost — by
nearly 10 points.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251116_2_leavitt.KwjMAE&refcode=em20251116-3498&amount=5&t=1003&akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt ][IMG]

But that campaign gave her exactly what she needed: a test run for the
MAGA talking points she now repeats from behind the White House podium.
She’s been practicing the spin for years — and while New Hampshire wasn’t
buying it, Trump was.

Let me be clear: I don’t regret a thing about standing up to Karoline and
her MAGA agenda — not then and not now.

This race is going to be tough. National Republicans and John Sununu are
doing everything they can to flip this seat. They’re recycling the same
extremist ideas, running the same playbook and relying on people like
Karoline Leavitt to carry their message.

We beat her once and with your help, we’ll do it again.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251116_2_leavitt.KwjMAE&refcode=em20251116-3498&amount=5&t=1004&akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt ]Will
you chip in $5 today to help us keep this Senate seat blue
and fight for what matters to Granite Staters?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251116_2_leavitt.KwjMAE&amount=5&refcode=em20251116-3498&t=1023&akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt ]Donate $5 »

Thanks for being in this fight with me.

Chris Pappas

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.chrispappas.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=3498%2E582294%2EX_yBbt

Hi, it’s Nick Naglic, Finance Director here at Gottheimer for Congress. I wanted to reach you personally before our mid-month deadline tonight at midnight.

Far-right extremists will stop at nothing to take this seat; we already have two opponents that have entered the race. With now less than a year until Election Day, it’s never been more important for us to start building our resources NOW to take them on.

That’s where you come in. Can you make a donation before midnight to stand with Josh and help us build for next year? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jg-fr-2511?amount=]

Nick Naglic

Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. clean_body: [#] Team, Hi, it’s Nick Naglic, Finance Director here at Gottheimer for Congress. I wanted to reach you personally before our mid-month deadline tonight at midnight. Far-right extremists will stop at nothing to take this seat; we already have two opponents that have entered the race. With now less than a year until Election Day, it’s never been more important for us to start building our resources NOW to take them on. That’s where you come in. Can you make a donation before midnight to stand with Josh and help us build for next year? [=] Thank you for your support, Nick Naglic Finance Director Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. If you'd like to support his campaign click here [] , if you'd like to unsubscribe click here. [] Thank you for being on Team Gottheimer. name: Senator Vincent Hughes
subject: New Funding for Pennsylvania Public Schools
body:
If you are having trouble viewing this message or would like to share it on a social network, you can [view the message online](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?gpiv=2100170997.172929.726&gen=1).
| Share On | [](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=share&piv=2100170997.172929.726&snid=2)
---|---
[](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100170997.172929.726&gen=1&mailing_linkid=16105)
#
# **New Funding for Pennsylvania Public Schools**
_Pennsylvania 's new state budget includes an over $500 million dollar investment to make sure all students receive a 21st century education, regardless of their zip code _ Pennsylvania's budget passed on Wednesday with an $18 billion commitment to funding schools and the education of our students. [Senator Hughes worked hard](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100170997.172929.726&gen=1&mailing_linkid=16107) to make sure this year's budget continued to fix Pennsylvania's broken education funding system and take steps to make all Pennsylvania public schools adequately and constitutionally funded.
* * *
**" We will continue to deliver on our Commonwealth Court ordered obligation to adequately and constitutionally fund our public schools. Fixing Pennsylvania's broken school funding system is one of the legislature's most important responsibilities. I'm proud Democrats remain committed to fighting for that funding."** **\- Senator Hughes -**
* * *
Overall, Pennsylvania's new state budget includes many different streams of important education funding that will support students, teachers, and administrators. Multiple parts of the new budget will equip Pennsylvania schools with more money for teachers, learning tools, mental health support, healthier environments, and resources for special education. [Continue to follow us on social media](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100170997.172929.726&gen=1&mailing_linkid=16108) to keep up with the latest that's happening in Harrisburg and right here in Philadelphia and Montgomery County.
* * *
| Offices of Senator Vincent Hughes

| | |
---

2401 North 54th Street
Philadelphia, PA 19131

Fax: 215.879.7778
| |

Harrisburg, PA 17120-3007

name: Paul Begala
subject: we’d be foolish to sleep on Iowa
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I’ve been in the game a long time, so I’ve got a sense which races are going to be close and which candidates are worth getting behind.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97462?refcode=em20251116-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit)
Peter, it’s political strategist Paul Begala.
I’ve been in the game a long time, so I’ve got a sense which races are going to be close and which candidates are worth getting behind.
I’m also very familiar with the importance of Iowa, which is why I know that Rob Sand has what it takes to win the governor’s race, for a number of reasons:
> _It’s already close. Like, really close. In fact, Sabato’s Crystal Ball recently moved it from “Likely R” to “Lean R.”_
> _The partisan insiders who’ve been at the wheel for years are historically unpopular. Before announcing she’s not running for re-election, Kim Reynolds was among the least popular governors in the country._
> _Rob Sand is a uniquely perfect candidate for this race, and his campaign gives Democrats a shot to win a race that would otherwise be out of reach._
**We’d be foolish to sleep on Iowa, and we’ve got an opportunity to make massive inroads in a tough state, but only if we support Rob Sand.[Will you please split a donation between his campaign to be Iowa’s next governor and Heartland Patriots?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97462?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97462?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit)**
Rob Sand gives us a chance to move Iowa forward and pry control away from the partisan insiders and special interests that he’s gone after during his time as State Auditor.
_Some of those same insiders are even running against Rob, too._
Winning in a state like Iowa won’t be easy. It’s going to require broad, far-ranging outreach, tough conversations with folks who might not always agree with us, and forging connections based on Iowa and the needs of the community.
But the key ingredient to winning a tough race in a challenging environment is grassroots support and enthusiasm. **_And that’s Rob’s not-so-secret weapon._**
**I’m all-in on helping elect Rob as Iowa’s next governor, but he’s going to need more than just my help — _he needs yours, too, Peter._ [Can he count on you to split a $25 donation to boost his campaign and help elect a governor who will move Iowa forward?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97462?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit)**
If you have stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Rob Sand and Heartland Patriots will process instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=5&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=6&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=7&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=8&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=9&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97463?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=10&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97462?refcode=em20251116-9715&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=11&akid=9715%2E614464%2EPoOBit)

We’ve got a rare opportunity in Iowa, and it’s up to all of us to make the most of it.
Thanks,
Paul Begala

clean_body: I’ve been in the game a long time, so I’ve got a sense which races are going to be close and which candidates are worth getting behind. Peter, it’s political strategist Paul Begala. I’ve been in the game a long time, so I’ve got a sense which races are going to be close and which candidates are worth getting behind. I’m also very familiar with the importance of Iowa, which is why I know that Rob Sand has what it takes to win the governor’s race, for a number of reasons: It’s already close. Like, really close. In fact, Sabato’s Crystal Ball recently moved it from “Likely R” to “Lean R.” The partisan insiders who’ve been at the wheel for years are historically unpopular. Before announcing she’s not running for re-election, Kim Reynolds was among the least popular governors in the country. Rob Sand is a uniquely perfect candidate for this race, and his campaign gives Democrats a shot to win a race that would otherwise be out of reach. We’d be foolish to sleep on Iowa, and we’ve got an opportunity to make massive inroads in a tough state, but only if we support Rob Sand. Rob Sand gives us a chance to move Iowa forward and pry control away from the partisan insiders and special interests that he’s gone after during his time as State Auditor. Some of those same insiders are even running against Rob, too. Winning in a state like Iowa won’t be easy. It’s going to require broad, far-ranging outreach, tough conversations with folks who might not always agree with us, and forging connections based on Iowa and the needs of the community. But the key ingredient to winning a tough race in a challenging environment is grassroots support and enthusiasm. And that’s Rob’s not-so-secret weapon. I’m all-in on helping elect Rob as Iowa’s next governor, but he’s going to need more than just my help — he needs yours, too, Peter. If you have stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Rob Sand and Heartland Patriots will process instantly: We’ve got a rare opportunity in Iowa, and it’s up to all of us to make the most of it. Thanks, Paul Begala | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

subject: Your thoughts on a national vote-by-mail system?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
You are invited to join our one-click response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
Mail-in balloting has been proven safe and secure after broad utilization in the last three elections. Opponents of voting rights are still working to restrict it to protect their own power in states across the country, but a solution has been proposed: A national vote-by-mail law passed by Congress.
We want to know your feelings on the topic. Will you take our one-question poll below?
Team: Are you in favor of a federal vote-by-mail law that allows for mail-in voting nationwide?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=111625_1yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=111625_1no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=111625_1&amount=20]

# [District Round‐up 11.16.25](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2025%2F11%2F16%2Fdistrict-round-up-11-16-25%2F%3Fjetpack_skip_subscription_popup&sr=0&signature=8c13f8f75720a7d828ed61826da99a3d&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
| By **Elizabeth Farnham** on November 16, 2025
---
Dear Neighbor,
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-fa,rnham-for-congress-1](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=52a630d9a6652dc4b78bbf7fc128797f6cac80f48f0522168ceec174fbf84c88&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5560&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=cb03959bc2c7cacff1fbf84a6c478a03&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=18c11560979c7597d84357c3793c67b9)
Now that nearly all results from the November 4th election have been reported, I've been able to notice a few encouraging patterns. Of great note is the pattern of decreased Republican vote share, compared to that of 2024 for races at the top of the ticket (Trump - 2024, Neuman - 2025) across the District. Including information from the ten full counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, and Perry, but not that of the partial counties of Somerset and Cumberland, the average decrease of Republican vote share equaled about 13 points. This was apparent especially where Democrats won contested elections, like Chad-Alan Carr for Mayor of Gettysburg, the Reverend Sylvia King for Mayor of Johnstown and startlingly - in Chambersburg where Lu Butler, Andrew Kilbride, David Wilson, and Curtis Shoup flipped four incumbant Republican seats, changing their borough council makeup from 8 Republicans and 2 Democrats to 5 and 5! In fact, the biggest swings happened in three out of four of our most populated districts.
Of lesser note, but still encouraging, the average voter turnout of the ten full counties increased by 3.8% when we compare it to the municipal election of 2023.
While we still have a ways to go, votes are moving in the right direction. And, it seems, the voters are more engaged.
Wednesday, November 12th, 2025, was a very busy news day. On that day:
The longest partial government shutdown in US history ended.
The 4 month Pennsylvania state budget impasse ended.
After a Republican-imposed 50 day wait, US Representative Adelita Grijalva (D-AZ) was sworn into Congress.
After being sworn in, Congresswoman Grijalva provided the 218th petition signature needed to bring a vote to the US House floor to release the Epstein files.
Emails from pedofile Jeffrey Epstein that repeatedly refer to Trump's knowledge of his pal Epstein sexually assaulting children were released by some members of Congress.
For many there were mixed feelings, especially concerning the seven Democratic senators who changed their minds and voted for the Republican resolution. In my opinion, those particular Democrats betrayed all of the sacrifices of those who had held the line the entire time. I recognize that the starvation of Americans could not continue, but the American people clearly recognized Republican cruelty through their election rebuke. I believe "scabs" is too kind a term for them, but I do understand that there are many feelings to sort through.
On one hand, Americans in need are more likely to receive their food assistance, federal workers will be paid for their labor, and air travel will be less fraught. On the other hand, Trump and his sick cronies, including our own US Representative, multimillionaire John Joyce, have learned that hungry Americans can be used as pawns to deny help to other Americans. With a convicted felon running our country and a Republican Congress ceding all of their power to him, all of us lose.
The biggest losers will be those who need Affordable Care Act (ACA, Obamacare, Pennie) subsidies to continue but will only experience skyrocketing premiums when instead, those subsidies end this year. The Republicans pinky-promised they would revisit the issue in December, but only a fool would believe them now.
The next group of losers are the rest of the American people as the continuing resolution for most agencies will only be funded through January 30th of next year. That will get us through the holidays, but then, who knows? At least Supplemental Nutritional Assistance Program (SNAP) is on paper to be funded through next September, the hemorraging of our federal workers is supposed to stop, and those federal workers who hadn't been paid are entitled to backpay through the deal that passed the House. However, with the Trump administration fighting as hard as it did to fund SNAP during the shutdown, I do not trust him as far as I can throw him to ensure SNAP remains funded.
At least the state of Pennsylvania passed its budget. The clear winner was Public Education - congratulations to Susan Spicka and her team of Education Voters of PA! Public school districts will send $178 million less to cyber schools and the poorest public schools will receive $565 million to close an "adequacy gap."
Unfortunately, the Educational Improvedment Tax Credit (EITC) will increase to $590 million to underwrite scholarships for Pennsylvania students to attend private schools which have neither the transparency, nor the accountability that public schools have.
Sadly, Pennsylvania ended its participation in the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative that capped carbon pollution.
Lastly, "Pennsylvanians will be able to claim the state's Working Pennsylvanian's Tax Credit if they claim the federal tax credit in the same year."
Thank you to everyone who is looking out for Everyday Americans. Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=3e415355a871ec9f063482f044ca46ce9003853119992146445637419221cc3f&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5560&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=c02dbc5ebac58a42a86becb3f858cb4f&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=18c11560979c7597d84357c3793c67b9)!

On Saturday, Team Beth Farnham for Congress decorated a campaign tree at the Franklin County Visitors Bureau for the Festival of Trees. Soon, community members will be able to bid on it and others to take home or to their place of business. Proceeds benefit the Cumberland Valley Breast Care Alliance. Many, many thanks to team members Sandy, Jack, Maribeth, and Ike for working so hard.
If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism.
We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you.
Sincerely,
Beth

clean_body: | Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-... | or | | | or ---|---|--- | By Elizabeth Farnham on November 16, 2025 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: Now that nearly all results from the November 4th election have been reported, I've been able to notice a few encouraging patterns. Of great note is the pattern of decreased Republican vote share, compared to that of 2024 for races at the top of the ticket (Trump - 2024, Neuman - 2025) across the District. Including information from the ten full counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, and Perry, but not that of the partial counties of Somerset and Cumberland, the average decrease of Republican vote share equaled about 13 points. This was apparent especially where Democrats won contested elections, like Chad-Alan Carr for Mayor of Gettysburg, the Reverend Sylvia King for Mayor of Johnstown and startlingly - in Chambersburg where Lu Butler, Andrew Kilbride, David Wilson, and Curtis Shoup flipped four incumbant Republican seats, changing their borough council makeup from 8 Republicans and 2 Democrats to 5 and 5! In fact, the biggest swings happened in three out of four of our most populated districts. Of lesser note, but still encouraging, the average voter turnout of the ten full counties increased by 3.8% when we compare it to the municipal election of 2023. While we still have a ways to go, votes are moving in the right direction. And, it seems, the voters are more engaged. Wednesday, November 12th, 2025, was a very busy news day. On that day: The longest partial government shutdown in US history ended. The 4 month Pennsylvania state budget impasse ended. After a Republican-imposed 50 day wait, US Representative Adelita Grijalva (D-AZ) was sworn into Congress. After being sworn in, Congresswoman Grijalva provided the 218th petition signature needed to bring a vote to the US House floor to release the Epstein files. Emails from pedofile Jeffrey Epstein that repeatedly refer to Trump's knowledge of his pal Epstein sexually assaulting children were released by some members of Congress. For many there were mixed feelings, especially concerning the seven Democratic senators who changed their minds and voted for the Republican resolution. In my opinion, those particular Democrats betrayed all of the sacrifices of those who had held the line the entire time. I recognize that the starvation of Americans could not continue, but the American people clearly recognized Republican cruelty through their election rebuke. I believe "scabs" is too kind a term for them, but I do understand that there are many feelings to sort through. On one hand, Americans in need are more likely to receive their food assistance, federal workers will be paid for their labor, and air travel will be less fraught. On the other hand, Trump and his sick cronies, including our own US Representative, multimillionaire John Joyce, have learned that hungry Americans can be used as pawns to deny help to other Americans. With a convicted felon running our country and a Republican Congress ceding all of their power to him, all of us lose. The biggest losers will be those who need Affordable Care Act (ACA, Obamacare, Pennie) subsidies to continue but will only experience skyrocketing premiums when instead, those subsidies end this year. The Republicans pinky-promised they would revisit the issue in December, but only a fool would believe them now. The next group of losers are the rest of the American people as the continuing resolution for most agencies will only be funded through January 30th of next year. That will get us through the holidays, but then, who knows? At least Supplemental Nutritional Assistance Program (SNAP) is on paper to be funded through next September, the hemorraging of our federal workers is supposed to stop, and those federal workers who hadn't been paid are entitled to backpay through the deal that passed the House. However, with the Trump administration fighting as hard as it did to fund SNAP during the shutdown, I do not trust him as far as I can throw him to ensure SNAP remains funded. At least the state of Pennsylvania passed its budget. The clear winner was Public Education - congratulations to Susan Spicka and her team of Education Voters of PA! Public school districts will send $178 million less to cyber schools and the poorest public schools will receive $565 million to close an "adequacy gap." Unfortunately, the Educational Improvedment Tax Credit (EITC) will increase to $590 million to underwrite scholarships for Pennsylvania students to attend private schools which have neither the transparency, nor the accountability that public schools have. Sadly, Pennsylvania ended its participation in the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative that capped carbon pollution. Lastly, "Pennsylvanians will be able to claim the state's Working Pennsylvanian's Tax Credit if they claim the federal tax credit in the same year." Thank you to everyone who is looking out for Everyday Americans. Together, we ! Sources: On Saturday, Team Beth Farnham for Congress decorated a campaign tree at the Franklin County Visitors Bureau for the Festival of Trees. Soon, community members will be able to bid on it and others to take home or to their place of business. Proceeds benefit the Cumberland Valley Breast Care Alliance. Many, many thanks to team members Sandy, Jack, Maribeth, and Ike for working so hard. If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. name: Talarico for Texas
subject: There’s magic in even $1.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter --
We set a goal to raise 5,000 donations this weekend. We’re halfway to the FEC deadline, and this is what it takes to make sure we stay on track to reach our goals and make the early investments required to flip Texas.
Our goal is focused on the number of donations because that number is really important to James. It shows that people are fueling this movement — not corporate PACs or the billionaires who buy politicians in Texas.
[**But we haven’t hit our goal yet, so we’re lowering our ask to just $1. What do you say? Please use this link if you can add $1 tonight.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251116-2-ak&t=1&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe)
[CONTRIBUTE $1](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251116-2-ak&t=2&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe)
It may not seem like enough to make a difference. But this is the magic of our movement.
When lots of people give small amounts of money, it adds up in powerful ways.
Each donation is another person who shares our values, raising their hand to be a part of our fight.
It’s another person sending a message to the corporations and billionaires who try to buy elections that this U.S. Senate seat is not for sale.
The people are standing up to take back Texas.
[**Please contribute $1 to say you have James Talarico’s back and power our campaign for the U.S. Senate.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251116-2-ak&t=3&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe)
[CONTRIBUTE $1](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251116-2-ak&t=4&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe)
Team Talarico

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update your contact information, [**you can submit this form.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/update-info/?t=5&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe) Looking for Team Talarico merch? [**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. [**Please consider making a contribution to support our fight today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jt-em-launch?refcode=em_footer&t=6&refcode2=235_1347274_wTdlJe&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe)
James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=7&akid=235%2E1347274%2EwTdlJe).

name: Roy Cooper for Senate
subject: Why Roy’s REALLY running for Senate…
body:
(not asking for money)
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1898?t=1&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
Good afternoon, Peter! Team Cooper here, back with more updates from the road — and things have been _busy._ | But first, we have an update for you: **We have a new phone number!** 📲 Make sure you’re getting our messages by **texting JOIN to 56801.**
---
Read on to check out what Roy’s been up to. 👇
* * *
**ROY ON THE ROAD**
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2013?t=6&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
_Meeting with North Carolina’s farmers, who’ve gotten a bad deal thanks to the national GOP’s tariffs._
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2014?t=7&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
_Republicans in Washington have been trying to rip away health care from North Carolinians. Roy’s running for Senate to fix things, not break them._
* * *
**QUESTION OF THE DAY**
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2015?t=8&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
_Roy’s running to serve in the U.S. Senate — but also, as a true Carolina Hurricanes fan, he’s got another motive to spend some time in Washington: undermining the Capitals, Washington’s NHL team and a Canes rival._
**[What about you, Peter? What’s the North Carolina team you can’t stop watching? Let Roy know!👇](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/Share_Sports_Team?t=9&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
**[REP YOUR TEAM](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/Share_Sports_Team?t=10&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
---
* * *
**KEY CAMPAIGN UPDATES**
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2017?t=11&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
_North Carolina’s ready to get to work and win._
Peter, somehow, there are only 353 days left between today and Election Day 2026.
We’re planning to spend every single one connecting with voters and building the support we need to win for North Carolina. **[With less than a year left, we’re asking: will you endorse Roy today?](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/Endorsement?t=12&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
**[I ENDORSE ROY](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/Endorsement?t=13&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
---
* * *
That’s all for now. See you again next time!
— Team Cooper
**FOLLOW ALONG:**
**[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2019?t=14&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2020?t=15&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2021?t=16&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2022?t=17&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2023?t=18&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
---|---|---|---|---
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=2&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=3&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?t=4&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=5&akid=47%2E124072%2EILyjAn)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602

The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

Dear Fellow American, In the beginning, God created male and female.

The Bible states there are only two genders. Each gender is equal in value, but different in design.

Some people in media and government would have you believe otherwise. They have rejected what the Bible says and adopted a lie.

Now, they're pushing gender ideology on you and the rest of the world. These lies can cause confusion and harm to you and your loved ones.

Despite what some in the media or government may have you believe, the majority of American adults say only two genders exist.

Do you agree there are only two genders?

clean_body: In the beginning, God created male and female. Dear Fellow American, In the beginning, God created | | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this Dear Fellow American, In the beginning, God created male and female. The Bible states there are only two genders. Each gender is equal in value, but different in design. Some people in media and government would have you believe otherwise. They have rejected what the Bible says and adopted a lie. Now, they're pushing gender ideology on you and the rest of the world. These lies can cause confusion and harm to you and your loved ones. Despite what some in the media or government may have you believe, the majority of American adults say only two genders exist. Do you agree there are only two genders? Click here to tell us how you feel. Liberty and justice for all, Jon Scruggs Senior Counsel, Vice President of Litigation Strategy & the Center for Conscience Initiatives Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260 .

clean_body: Final notice before we close the books on 2025. We've reached out a few times Fellow American about the change in your membership status and we really hope you understand what's at stake. According to our records, your has NOT been activated. Our team members are working day and night to defend Conservative values nationwide and are fighting to stop the Radical Left from destroying our nation! | REPUBLICAN STATE LEADERSHIP MEMBERSHIP NOTICE ACCOUNT HOLDER: 2024 MEMBERSHIP: LAPSED 2025 MEMBERSHIP ENROLLMENT: PENDING Republican State Leadership is counting on you to act NOW to stand with us as we put an end to the Left's radical agenda! By renewing your membership, you are sending the loudest possible message to the Radical Left and making it CLEAR that you proudly stand with Republican State Leadership nationwide! Thank you, Republican State Leadership Membership Services * By providing your mobile phone number, you are consenting to receive calls and texts and P2P messages, including autodialed and automated calls and texts, to that number from RSLC and its affiliated PACs. Message frequency may vary. Msg&Data Rates May Apply. Reply STOP to cancel. Reply HELP for help. Contributions or gifts to the Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) and its affiliated PACs are not tax deductible. Contributions may be used for activities of the RSLC and its affiliated PACs at the RSLC's sole discretion. The RSLC does not accept earmarked contributions. name: =?utf-8?q?with_a_heavy_heart=E2=80=A6_via_Fight_For_Reform?=
subject: we’re heartbroken… please help >>
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23623%2E4449941%2EmW_V_p) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23623%2E4449941%2EmW_V_p) Team, we’re writing to you with a heavy heart today.

[**We’re devastated — truly heartbroken — because something deeply WRONG is happening in our country.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70055?t=3&akid=23623%2E4449941%2EmW_V_p)

In 2024, Donald Trump was convicted on 34 felony counts...
Yet he still became President.

Now he’s selling out our government to Dark Money billionaires, cozying up to war criminals, and tightening his grip on Washington.

But top Democrats are fighting back with the _No Kings Act_ — a historic bill that would strip Donald Trump of immunity once and for all!

🔵Adam Schiff
🔵Elizabeth Warren
🔵Chuck Schumer
🔵Raphael Warnock

# [Cliff Schecter & Sam Drzymala - When the Old Guard Falls, the People Rise](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178809040&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4ODA5MDQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjMzMzkzMjAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTkzMTMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NngXdd83789AsISLn9iqjzQ5ocvtXcj9lP5Kk1Jn1dM)
### Cliff breaks down his explosive conversation with Sam Drzymala on betrayal, backlash, and the future of democracy.
| | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge), [Sam Drzymala](https://substack.com/@samdrzymala), and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Nov 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@samdrzymala)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178809040&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4ODA5MDQwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MzMzOTMyMCwiZXhwIjoxNzY1OTMxMzIwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.feB-k6xGHu1avXmYXkjhrTINtf-rcg_sKhPGVVxfK2Q&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178809040&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4ODA5MDQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjMzMzkzMjAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTkzMTMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NngXdd83789AsISLn9iqjzQ5ocvtXcj9lP5Kk1Jn1dM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178809040&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4ODA5MDQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjMzMzkzMjAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTkzMTMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NngXdd83789AsISLn9iqjzQ5ocvtXcj9lP5Kk1Jn1dM)
---
| | [](https://substack..kHmy91VufHtA8ZvQefejHEottMy7XjhxdjDPlLO4T5g?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/cliff-schecter-and-sam-drzymala-live?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7eb626d5-49d3-42d0-bb4e-ee75e387bc99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Hey folks, Cliff here -- and if you missed my conversation with [Sam Drzymala](https://open.substack.com/users/13168097-sam-drzymala?utm_source=mentions) this week, buckle up, because we went deep into the swamp rot, revival, and raw potential of American democracy. This wasn't a "show," per se -- it was two people who've lived inside the political machine stepping outside of it to tell you exactly why it's breaking... and exactly how we fix it.
And yes, we got loud because the moment demands it.
We started with the betrayal -- the eight Democratic Senators who folded like lawn chairs left out in a hurricane. Sam and I walked through how the collapse happened, why the excuses were insulting, and why the _real_ reason they caved is the same tired story: an old guard clinging to power by protecting the broken systems that produced them.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a9d8c6e7-5e56-45cf-9c2e-2d360b533613?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
They're not strategizing for the future -- they're fighting to preserve the past. A past that no longer works.
But here's the good news: the future is already here. And it's pissed off.
Because the grassroots is awake and punching back.
* * *
Subscribe for free to build this movement!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.nZHYvIf7wLAWqJFw6RF4yhRUHHY-dGxeRFyISsvgQj0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178809040)
* * *
We talked about how YOU -- the audience, the subscribers, the activists, the creators -- are the beating heart of a new democratic movement. Not the legacy media. Not the consultants who still think we're campaigning like it's 1998. Not even the electeds who think leadership means reading talking points written by someone who hasn't spoken to an actual voter since the MySpace era.
No -- you're driving the change.
We dug into election night: the overperformances, the sweeps, the generational surge happening not in D.C. but in state legislatures, school boards, and forgotten districts. The places where democracy actually lives. I walked Sam through why what you saw on Tuesday wasn't a fluke -- it was a pattern. A big one. A wave that only breaks bigger if we keep feeding it.
I also pointed folks toward David Shuster's essential wrap-up on the **218-signature discharge petition** -- because if you want a masterclass in how Democrats _can_ fight when they choose to, read **[Shuster's Weekly Wrap-Up](https://substack.com/redirect/e7f87d70-2f3f-4b77-8e93-c2ab0c3e00ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
And if you want to understand why information itself is a battlefield now, and how the right floods the zone with chaos, you need **[But His Emails!](https://substack.com/redirect/f64d4f65-2eb7-4f20-94eb-b83db7c43055?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** -- a breakdown of the GOP's propaganda machine and how we counter it with Egberto Willies.
Sam and I also got into the core truth of this moment: the old "broadcast model" is dead. The new power structure is decentralized, interactive, creator-driven, and impossible to gatekeep. You talk directly to us; we talk directly to you. And together we build something the old system can't contain -- or control.
So yeah, there was frustration. But also hope. Real hope. Earned hope.
Because the Eight may have caved.
But millions of us haven't.
And we're the ones shaping what comes next.
Stay loud. Stay engaged. Stay in the fight.
--Cliff
PS: If you're not amped up enough already, check out my latest on [Eric Trump: The Patron Saint of the Stupid Rich.](https://substack.com/redirect/db2aa885-54f0-49ba-93b9-59fff65e1360?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e19d6ddc-a850-4635-b96c-ca964b0974e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/184e3c03-b022-4d33-b7cc-dd137fc879dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| A guest post by| [Sam Drzymala](https://substack.com/@samdrzymala?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)(he/they) I write about politics, media, philosophy, history, and masculinity through a mythical, spiritual lens.[Subscribe to Sam](https://substack.com/redirect/1f2d7e8d-a8c5-476d-bff8-c6146a07c601?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

---
| | [Comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178809040&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4ODA5MDQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjMzMzkzMjAsImV4cCI6MTc2NTkzMTMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NngXdd83789AsISLn9iqjzQ5ocvtXcj9lP5Kk1Jn1dM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

name: West Wing Daily
subject: You won't believe how the Democrats just got their Heads Handed to Them
body:
Trump’s enemies will do anything to get in the way of his agenda to Make America Great Again. [](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xtl=alnjksg334o7b74f6q54oykjkwmns2hs5x207mzwmp25mdprzplrgt345dr4g71pxmmnrp217dv3ljr1okbxmygt0wefkp4p46acx6iy3wuwvt0jirqgw6hy4sbcugjtf8nzkesr73vf3rdpjzsc9bl7ejgpuiyglgzgquvu2ng6az9zervwp52ahdq3p0ws41nsmsu381uwtnor3vfazwjtyiiwtsr7z13h8l086s0z7uz5jbnfvoxy5q&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5ueli&__onlt=h&ocx_contact_id=671c014947bb0347178b4ae6)
---
---
[Mark as Spam](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xul=65a6x6pv0ysxb8fw4oowqadn7i1yysjyfydmfm4y2q75ru89py6cze79qtdl7ru5fjhpszw4lei2ama9zn1ytnnv8gw6yrepk&__stmp=t5ueli&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) | [Unsubscribe](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xul=65a6x6pv0ysxb8fw4oowqadn7i1yysjyfydmfm4y2q75ru89py6cze79qtdl7ru5fjhpszw4lei2ama9zn1ytnnv8gw6yrepk&__stmp=t5ueli&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
* * *
Trump’s enemies will do anything to get in the way of his agenda to Make America Great Again. name: Jon Ossoff
subject: re: doomscrolling
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter, it’s Jon.
I completely understand how you might be feeling right now.
_The fear._
_The apprehension._
Donald Trump is weaponizing the entire federal government as a political instrument. And he’s staging a power grab unprecedented in our nation’s history.
But, Peter, we do not have the luxury of despair.
We can’t sit back and doomscroll. Or wait in vain for some charismatic savior to swoop in and save us.
We need to stay focused on what is in our control:
As we look ahead to the midterms, I think about the _generational_ obligation we have to win.
We cannot lose faith in our ability as citizens to use our Constitutional rights to shape this country's future. To save our Republic. And that starts right now.
**I am going to work like hell to win next year and restore checks and balances on this administration. But my race in Georgia is likely to be the most expensive U.S. Senate race in history. I cannot win it alone.**
[**In 2021, we rallied together and proved the pundits wrong to secure this seat in Georgia — and the Democratic Senate majority with it. Now we are facing a race just as critical. Can I count on you to chip in $10, or whatever you are able, to ensure we are able to WIN decisively next year?**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251116-1-subs&t=1&akid=1097%2E5691347%2EmecQNt)

Thank you,
Jon Ossoff

body:
| | | Patriot, The 2025 election should wake every American up. The Democrats aren’t even hiding it. They’re trying to dismantle capitalism, reward violence against free speech, and rewrite what it means to be American. _**We can’t stay silent.**_ That’s why I’m launching a nationwide petition to prove that Americans are ready to stand against communism and defend freedom before it’s too late.

name: Haley Stevens
subject: making sure you got my note, null
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I'm humbly asking one more time:

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
null, I wanted to quickly follow up to make sure you got my note.
---
I'm still looking for another 80 grassroots Democrats to personally endorse my campaign to become Michigan's next senator, and**I noticed you haven't had a chance to add your name yet.**
**That's why I'm humbly asking one more time:[Will you personally endorse my campaign to keep Michigan blue, take back the Senate for Democrats, and put a stop to Trump's disastrous agenda?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8754?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=2&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8754?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
---
Thank you for your support. It means the world to have you by my side in this race.

Haley Stevens
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Haley Stevens**
Subject: What I'm hearing from folks like you
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8755?t=4&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
---
null,
On the campaign trail, I've been talking directly with Michiganders across the state – folks like you who are concerned for the future – and **here's what I've been hearing:**
🛟 People are worried about the future of Medicare and Social Security.

🩺 Folks in Michigan and across the country want improved and affordable access to health care.

💰 Working families need real action to create and protect good-paying jobs.

⛓️💥 And many expressed deep concerns about the future of our fundamental freedoms, civil rights, and American democracy itself.
These aren’t just political talking points, null. They’re _real_ concerns from _real_ people. And they’re exactly why I’m running for Michigan's battleground Senate seat – **because I have a track record of getting things done.**
📌 I helped stabilize the auto industry and save over 200,000 Michigan jobs as chief of staff on President Obama's Auto Rescue Task Force.
📌 I played a leading role in passing the bill to grow our manufacturing economy by bringing America’s semiconductor industry back to where it belongs: Michigan.
📌 Most recently, I introduced the Stop Trump's Abuse of Power Act – a bill to ban the president from deploying active duty troops on U.S. soil without consent from state or local leaders.
That barely scratches the surface, and I know there's a _lot_ more work to do. So it's a good thing I grew up the Michigan way: surrounded by grease, grit, and hard work. And I'm in this race now so I can continue fighting for Michigan's working families in the U.S. Senate.
But with polling showing that I'm the Democrat with the very best shot at beating MAGA Republican Mike Rogers, the GOP is getting scared – **and they're gearing up to spend millions to flip this seat red.**
**So, null, I need****you on my grassroots team to help me****keep up my track record of fighting for hardworking Michigan families.****[Will you join me by endorsing my campaign today? My team set an ambitious goal to get 15,000 signatures by midnight, and right now, I’m still 40% short.](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8754?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)**
| | [Add Your Name](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8754?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
---
Thank you so much for your support,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** Null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8754?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=7&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8755?t=8&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=9&akid=1361%2E49239%2EiZtGh_)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Amazon got $15.7B in tax breaks. That could lower ACA premiums for 2.4 million people.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
This rugged individualism never seems to apply when it comes to giant corporations and billionaires.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2708%2E4032634%2E_8a6FK)
---
Congressional Republicans love to preach a “pull yourself up by your bootstraps” mentality — until they’re talking to a billionaire. They love to champion an America that says “I’ve got mine, the rest of you are on your own” — but this doesn’t seem to apply to giant corporations who get government handouts. Take Trump and the GOP’s Big Ugly Bill, for example. Alphabet gets $17.9B in tax breaks. That money could pay for SNAP benefits for 7.5 million Americans. Amazon gets $15.7B. That could lower ACA premiums for 2.4 million people. Microsoft gets $12.5B. That could cover Medicaid for 3.8 million children. Oh, and we can’t forget how Trump was somehow able to dig up $40 billion to bail out Argentina’s far-right government. That $40 billion could have made child care affordable for 2 million American families. But for American families needing help? They say there’s no funding. I’m done being surprised by their hypocrisy, but I’m not done fighting back. Simply put, the rules work differently for different groups of people — and I’m doing everything in my power to put a stop to this rigged system and change this. [**Will you pitch in $28 or anything you can to power my fight to unrig our economy for working people? Your support allows me to use my voice in the Senate to advocate for big, structural changes that actually help families.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3224?amount=28&t=8&akid=2708%2E4032634%2E_8a6FK)
